panels.description: End to end request latency measured in seconds.
panels.title: E2E Request Latency

panels.description: Number of tokens processed per second
panels.title: Token Throughput

panels.description: Number of requests in RUNNING, WAITING, and SWAPPED state
panels.title: Scheduler State

panels.description: P50, P90, P95, and P99 TTFT latency in seconds.
panels.title: Time To First Token Latency

panels.description: Percentage of used cache blocks by AFM.
panels.title: Cache Utilization

panels.description: Number of finished requests by their finish reason: either an EOS token was generated or the max sequence length was reached.
panels.title: Finish Reason
Radix Prefix Cache Hit Rate
panels.description: AFM-native: prefix cache effectiveness over time.

panels.title: Batch Utilization (in-flight vs cap)
panels.description: AFM-native: batch slot utilization. cap=0 means serial mode (no BatchScheduler).

panels.title: Per-request Decode Speed
panels.description:
Tokens-per-decode-second, averaged over the rate window — the model's actual decoding speed during active generation. This matches the chat UI's per-message t/s and the in-webui dashboard's 'Decode rate (last)' tile.

Distinct from the Token Throughput panel above, which shows WALL-CLOCK tokens/sec (= per-request decode speed × duty cycle). Per-request decode speed is constant across load; wall-clock throughput scales with how busy afm is.

panels.title: Active HTTP Connections
panels.description: AFM-native: HTTP client connections currently being served, plus the all-time-high since server start. /metrics scrapes are excluded so a Prometheus poll doesn't show up as a connection.

panels.title: Prefix Cache Fill (radix)
panels.description: AFM-native: how full the radix prefix cache is. 1.0 = at capacity (default 64 entries). Distinct from the 'Cache Utilization' panel above which now shows broader GPU memory pressure.
Total in-engine time per request (queue + prefill + decode). p50 / p90 / p95 / p99 in seconds.
panels.title: Inference Time
Request Rate (started / completed / in-flight)
panels.description: Request lifecycle. Steady-state: started rate ≈ completed rate. The in-flight series is the gap between the two cumulative counters and should hover near 0 except during active generation. A persistent positive value would indicate abandoned requests or a stuck slot.

panels.title: Server Uptime
panels.description: Wall-clock seconds since afm process start. Useful for spotting recent restarts in a long timeline.
